techniques.evidence_quote: Threat actors used an old, unrecycled Amazon Web Services (AWS) access key to gain unauthorized access to the server
unauthorized activity in one of the data stores

threat actors breached about 35 million records with 'masked card data and card fingerprint'... Juspay did acknowledge the compromise of some data records containing non-anonymized, plain-text email and phone numbers

attack_chain.description: Attackers obtained and leveraged an old, unrecycled AWS access key to authenticate to Juspay's AWS environment, bypassing normal access controls.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Threat actors used an old, unrecycled Amazon Web Services (AWS) access key to gain unauthorized access to the server

attack_chain.description: Using the compromised AWS access key, attackers discovered and identified the data store(s) containing customer payment records, card data, email IDs, and phone numbers.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: unauthorized activity in one of the data stores

attack_chain.description: Attackers accessed and exfiltrated approximately 35 million records of masked card data, card fingerprints, as well as plain-text email addresses and phone numbers from the compromised data store.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: threat actors breached about 35 million records with 'masked card data and card fingerprint'... Juspay did acknowledge the compromise of some data records containing non-anonymized, plain-text email and phone numbers

initial_access_vector: Compromised old, unrecycled AWS IAM access key used to authenticate directly to the AWS environment
persistence_mechanism: None identified; attack appears to have been a one-time smash-and-grab using the stale access key before detection
privilege_escalation: Not described; the stale AWS access key appears to have had sufficient permissions to directly access the target data stores
